metadata.from: Bob (Robert E. Hanifen) — signed 'Bobby,' written in first person as a son to his father
metadata.to: James Edward Hanifen Sr. (Dad) — addressed to Hanifen Body & Paint Co., Des Moines, Iowa
metadata.location_written_from: Caserta, Italy (per envelope back; also consistent with reference to having a fine time in Italy)
metadata.location_sent_to: Hanifen Body & Paint Co., 904 Keo Way, Des Moines, Iowa
metadata.postmark: U.S. Army Postal Service, May 10, 1946, APO 612
metadata.envelope_notes: Sender address: Cpl. Robert E. Hanifen 12170600, 20 12th Ord. & Mnt. Co. BE, APO 528, c/o P.M. New York, N.Y. Envelope is American Red Cross stationery (Caserta, Italy noted on back flap). Form 539 noted in red top right. The back of the envelope shows partial text from the letter bleeding through or written on the flap.
people_mentioned: St. David (unclear — possibly a person's name or place)
metadata.mood_tone: Cheerful and casual; slightly apologetic about not writing more; optimistic about coming home; light-hearted about social life
metadata.legibility_notes: Handwriting is generally readable but some words are difficult to parse — 'St. David' is uncertain, the phrase about the Officer's Club connections is somewhat unclear, and a few words on page 2 required guessing. The back of the envelope shows partial bleed-through of letter text that is mostly illegible.
metadata.historical_context: Post-WWII occupation period in Italy; Bob is still stationed in Caserta, Italy (a major Allied headquarters) as of May 1946. Reference to a convoy being sent to Germany on the 26th of May reflects ongoing occupation duties. The war is over but troops remain stationed in Europe.

pages.transcription:
Return address (upper left):
Cpl. Robert E. Hanifen 12170600
20 12th Ord. & Mnt. Co. BE
APO 528
c/o P.M. New York, N.Y.

Addressed to:
Hanifen Body & Paint Co.
904 Keo Way
Des Moines, Iowa

Postmark: U.S. Army Postal [Service], May 10, 194[6], APO 612
Top right: FORM 539 (in red)

American Red Cross [letterhead with red cross symbol]
Caserta  Italy

[Partial bleed-through or writing on envelope flap — mostly illegible; fragments visible include: 'prob m W[?]', 'and d[?]', 'by [partly?]', 'Some of th[e?]']

[top center, written as arithmetic/calculation:]
1875
D
37.50 [?] 1

9 May 1946

Dear Dad

    How my dad today in good health I hope. I have been having a little trouble lately with my head. But the Doc said I had a head cold which was causing it to ache. So everything is okay. I have been having a fine time in Italy lately. Went Dancing last night at the Red Cross and dance with a sweet girl I know. Some of these don't look no more funnie than I. This women work at the Officer Club might get a few connections through that Department. And over here that help a hell of a lot. I made 16 the 3 of may. Hope I can get by the time

I come home. f. I stay with St. David [St. David's?] I am sure of it. They are sending a convoy to Germany the 26 of May. It will take @ 2 months for it to get there and back. Hope I can on it. I am inclosing a 41 dollar money order. I still have $14 to send home to be up with my pay back. Did you ever get the 39 dollar one I sent. If so so. I never have got to 5 one get. Can't use the pip for recreational any more. Well Dad I will write again soon I have been goofing off lately but I will get on the ball soon.

Lots of Love Your Son
                    Bobby

full_transcription:
[Envelope front]
Cpl. Robert E. Hanifen 12170600
20 12th Ord. & Mnt. Co. BE
APO 528
c/o P.M. New York, N.Y.

Hanifen Body & Paint Co.
904 Keo Way
Des Moines, Iowa

[Page 1]
9 May 1946

Dear Dad

    How my dad today in good health I hope. I have been having a little trouble lately with my head. But the Doc said I had a head cold which was causing it to ache. So everything is okay. I have been having a fine time in Italy lately. Went Dancing last night at the Red Cross and dance with a sweet girl I know. Some of these don't look no more funnie than I. This women work at the Officer Club might get a few connections through that Department. And over here that help a hell of a lot. I made 16 the 3 of may. Hope I can get by the time

[Page 2]
I come home. f. I stay with St. David I am sure of it. They are sending a convoy to Germany the 26 of May. It will take @ 2 months for it to get there and back. Hope I can on it. I am inclosing a 41 dollar money order. I still have $14 to send home to be up with my pay back. Did you ever get the 39 dollar one I sent. If so so. I never have got to 5 one get. Can't use the pip for recreational any more. Well Dad I will write again soon I have been goofing off lately but I will get on the ball soon.

Lots of Love Your Son
                    Bobby
summary: Written on May 9, 1946, from Caserta, Italy, this letter from Bob (Bobby) Hanifen to his father updates him on his health (a head cold causing headaches, now resolved), his social life at Red Cross dances, and his financial situation — he is enclosing a $41 money order and inquires whether a previous $39 money order was received. He mentions a convoy being sent to Germany on May 26th that he hopes to join, and expresses optimism about coming home soon. The tone is cheerful and casual, with Bob acknowledging he has been 'goofing off' on letter writing but promising to do better.
